metadata_utm.description: Default metadata.utm attribution block stamped by build_app_data when the caller does not supply metadata.utm. Caller-supplied metadata.utm (partial or full) fully replaces the default so downstream analytics receive the caller-declared attribution byte-identical.

cases.description: Sell-order posting uses quote fee plus slippage-adjusted buy amount.
Buy-order posting uses fee-inclusive plus slippage-adjusted sell amount while keeping buy amount fixed.
Quote requests enrich appData with appCode and slippage settings before submission.
Quote requests default to validFor 1800 seconds, allow exact validTo, and reject simultaneous validFor plus validTo.

expected.simultaneous_valid_for_and_valid_to_erro: Cannot specify both validFor and validTo. Use validFor for relative time or validTo for absolute time.

cases.description: Selling native ETH uses the wrapped native token address, EIP1271 onchain quote flags, and the chain default ETH flow slippage when slippage is omitted.
AUTO slippage adopts the suggested slippage when provided and otherwise falls back to the chain default.
Slippage helper outputs clamp to 0 for non-EthFlow negatives, clamp to the EthFlow minimum for EthFlow orders, and cap at 10000 bps.
Partner fee settings are copied into appData metadata before order submission.
Advanced quoteRequest overrides such as receiver and validTo propagate into the final order, and owner takes precedence over signer for the from and receiver fields.

Default-constructed OrderToSignParams applies cost, slippage, partner-fee, and protocol-fee adjustments when converting a quote into the unsigned order, matching the shipped internal quote and submission flows.
OrderToSignParams::new(...).with_apply_costs_slippage_and_fees(false) preserves the caller sell and buy amounts instead of folding cost, slippage, or fee adjustments into the unsigned order.
Limit-order posting delegates with applyCostsSlippageAndFees disabled.
Pre-sign transactions always have zero value, add a 20 percent gas margin, and honor settlementContractOverride before env defaults.
EthFlow transactions replace the sell token with wrapped native, set transaction value to the sell amount, and honor ethFlowContractOverride before env defaults.
Native-sell posting uploads appData, emits a transaction to send on chain, and supports duplicate-order callbacks for unique order id collision handling.
On-chain cancellation routes regular orders through settlement and EthFlow orders through the EthFlow contract while preserving fallback gas handling.
TradingSdk quote-only mode works without a signer when owner is explicit, uses owner as from, and raises explicit errors when chainId or appCode are missing.
Allowance reads and approval transaction creation remain explicit runtime-boundary helpers with call-level chainId resolution over trader defaults.
Call-level settlement and EthFlow overrides take precedence over trader-level overrides, which take precedence over env defaults.
